hero.question: Will Cleveland Guardians win the 2026 American League Championship Series?
hero.prob_take: At 9.4%, the market has priced Cleveland into the "teams that make the playoffs but have zero shot" bucket, which is where most sub-10% ALCS favorites belong. The Guardians actually have a real rotation and aren't the Yankees, so if they win the AL East or grab a wild card, they're more likely than the crowd thinks to beat one team in a five-gamer.

daily_take.headline: Guardians ALCS odds jumped 4.5pts. Here's why you should fade the move.
daily_take.deck: Cleveland's pennant chances spiked to 9.4% on $626K in volume, likely driven by their offseason moves and early betting season optimism. The market is overweighting roster improvements and underweighting the brutal ALCS gauntlet. At 9.4%, you're paying for a team that hasn't won a postseason series since 2016. The sharp play: fade the recency bias and take the under.
